g.text: Lots of conditionals but as far as "screens" are concerned
1. User Enters in Email or SMS
4. Display List of Keys with Provider Delegations w/New Key Option
5. Display Selection of Data to be sent to Provider
7. Display Permissions Acceptance Screen and Capture Data
which is really just a permutation of #6
This is just how I've mapped them for this document,
not dictating it be this way, merged or split apart up to design
Provider sends user who would like to SiWA request(publicKey,callbackUrl,permissions,nonce,signature)
User Enters in Email or SMS
There is a lot going on here
1. Choose the Existing Key to login with
2. Changing Permissions for this Provider on Existing Key
3. Add this Provider to Existing Key, potential migration
4. Add this Provider to a New Key, potential migration
- Migrations aren't really up to the User so those aren't
- Same with changing permissions, I think it's
- New Key is an overt interaction
- Adding to an existing key is an overt interaction
Display List of Keys with Provider Delegations w/New Key Option
Display Selection of Data to be sent to Provider
Redirect to Provider callbackUrl with authorizationCode
Add Delta Identifiers to FA Account
Display Selection of Data to be sent to Provider
Redirect to Provider callbackUrl with authorizationCode
Add Single Identifier to existing FA Account
Add Delta Identifiers to FA Account
Display Permissions Acceptance Screen and Capture Data
Display Selection of Data to be sent to Provider
Redirect to Provider callbackUrl with authorizationCode
Add Single Identifier to new FA Account
Some of the Others exist in FA, Delta Migration
None of the Other Identifiers Exist In Fa, New Migration
Add Delta Identifiers to Existing FA Account
Add All Identifiers to New FA Account
Display Permissions Acceptance Screen and Capture Data
Display Selection of Data to be sent to Provider
Redirect to Provider callbackUrl with authorizationCode
